title: Fetch Client Collection
description: A Fetch Client collection defines a group of HTTP requests along with their configuration, assertions, variable extractors, and pre-run request chains.

schemaVersion.description: The version of the Fetch Client export schema. Integer - importers gate on this with a numeric comparison (schemaVersion >= 2). Increment only on breaking structural changes.

items.description: Flat list of every folder and request in the collection. Reconstruct the tree by grouping items by parentId. Items without a parentId are root-level children. Sort siblings by their `order` field before rendering.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Identity and timestamp information for this export.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 datetime when the collection was first created. E.g: '2026-06-19T12:18:41.000Z'.

exportedAt.description: ISO-8601 datetime when this export file was generated.

generator.description: The application name and version that produced this file. E.g: 'Fetch Client 2.0'.

collection-settings.title: Collection Settings
collection-settings.description: Application-level defaults inherited by all root-level items unless they override them.

folder-defaults.title: Folder Defaults
folder-defaults.description: Values cascaded to all direct children of a folder unless a child overrides them. Semantically different from collection settings - these are inherited defaults, not application-level configuration.

folder.title: Folder
folder.description: A folder groups related requests. Folders can be nested by setting parentId to another folder's id. Root-level folders omit parentId.

type.description: Discriminator field. Always 'folder' for folder items.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 datetime when the folder was created.

order.description: 1-based position of this folder among its siblings. Importers must sort by this field - do not rely on array position.

parentId.description: ID of the parent folder. Omitted when the folder is at the collection root.

request.title: Request
request.description: A single HTTP request with its configuration, auth, body, assertions, variable extractors, and pre-run chain.

type.description: Discriminator field. Always 'request' for request items.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 datetime when the request was created.

order.description: 1-based position of this request among its siblings. Importers must sort by this field - do not rely on array position.

parentId.description: ID of the parent folder. Omitted when the request is at the collection root.

method.description: The HTTP method for this request. Always uppercase.

url.description: The full request URL including protocol, host, path and any inline path variables.

notes.description: Free-text notes or documentation for this request. Omitted when empty.

key-value.title: Key-Value
key-value.description: A single key-value pair used for headers, query params, form fields, and url-encoded fields.

key.description: The name / key of the pair.

enabled.description: When false, this pair is present in the export but will not be sent with the request.

description.description: Optional comment or description for this pair.

key-value-list.title: Key-Value List
key-value-list.description: An ordered list of key-value pairs. Empty rows are not exported.

auth.title: Auth
auth.description: Authentication configuration for a request, folder, or collection. Parse by switching on the `type` field - only the relevant credentials object is present.

type.description: The authentication type. 'inherit' means resolve auth from the parent folder or collection settings.

credentials.title: Auth Credentials
credentials.description: The credentials object. Shape varies by type - absent for 'noauth' and 'inherit'.

key.description: The header or query parameter name for the API key.

addTo.description: Where to attach the API key - as a request header or as a query parameter.

auth-aws-credentials.title: AWS Signature v4 Credentials
auth-aws-credentials.description: Credentials for AWS Signature Version 4 Authentication.

sessionToken.description: Optional temporary session token for STS-based credentials.

tokenName.description: A human-readable label for the token.

clientId.description: The client ID issued by the authorization server.

clientSecret.description: The client secret issued by the authorization server.

clientAuth.description: How client credentials are sent to the token endpoint.

username.description: Resource owner username. Only used with grantType 'password_credentials'.

password.description: Resource owner password. Only used with grantType 'password_credentials'.

audience.description: The intended audience of the token.

resource.description: The resource URI the token is requested for.

body.title: Request Body
body.description: The body of the HTTP request. Parse by switching on the `type` field - only the fields relevant to the active body type are present.

fields.description: Form fields. Present only when type is 'formdata' or 'urlencoded'.

content.description: The raw body content as a string. Present only when type is 'raw'.

language.description: Language hint for the raw body editor. E.g: 'json', 'xml', 'text', 'html', 'javascript'. Present only when type is 'raw'.

fileName.description: The name of the binary file to upload. Present only when type is 'binary'.

contentType.description: MIME type of the binary file, derived from the file extension. Present only when type is 'binary'.

contentTypeOption.description: 'auto' means the MIME type was derived from the file extension. 'manual' means it was user-overridden. Present only when type is 'binary'.

query.description: The GraphQL query string. Present only when type is 'graphql'.

variables.description: JSON string of the GraphQL variables object. Present only when type is 'graphql'.

assertion.title: Assertion
assertion.description: A single test assertion evaluated against the HTTP response. The `source` field identifies what is being tested; `path` provides a sub-path when the source requires it.

path.description: Required when source is 'headers.custom' (value = header name, e.g. 'X-Request-Id') or 'body.jsonPath' (value = JSONPath expression, e.g. '[0].id'). Omitted for all other sources.

expectedValue.description: The value to compare against. For actions that do not compare a value (e.g. 'isJSON'), pass an empty string.

assertion-list.title: Assertion List
assertion-list.description: An ordered list of test assertions evaluated after the response is received.

assertion-source.title: Assertion Source
assertion-source.description: Identifies the part of the response to test. Maps to internal ParametersModelMapping values.

assertion-action.title: Assertion Action
assertion-action.description: The comparison or evaluation operation to perform.

variable-extractor.title: Variable Extractor
variable-extractor.description: Extracts a value from the response and assigns it to a named variable for use in subsequent requests.

path.description: Required when source is 'headers.custom' (value = header name) or 'body.jsonPath' (value = JSONPath expression). Omitted for all other sources.

variableName.description: The name of the variable to assign the extracted value into.

variable-extractor-list.title: Variable Extractor List
variable-extractor-list.description: An ordered list of variable extractors evaluated after the response is received.

pre-run-request.title: Pre-Run Request
pre-run-request.description: A reference to another request in the collection that should be executed before the current request runs. An optional condition controls whether execution is skipped.

requestId.description: The ID of the request to execute. Must reference a valid request item within the same collection.

order.description: 1-based execution order among pre-run entries for this request.

condition.description: Optional run-condition. When present, the pre-run request executes only if this assertion evaluates to true against the current execution context. Uses the same shape as a test assertion.

pre-run-request-list.title: Pre-Run Request List
pre-run-request-list.description: An ordered list of request references to execute before the current request or as collection/folder-level setup.

collection-variables.title: Collection Variables
collection-variables.description: List of variables linked to the collection
